title: make_unique and make_shared should be preferred to construct unique_ptr and shared_ptr
description: make_unique and make_shared are more concise than explicitly calling the constructor of unique_ptr and shared_ptr since they don’t require specifying the type multiple times and eliminate the need to use new. make_unique and make_shared should also be preferred for exception-safety and performance reasons. While make_unique and make_shared are exception-safe, complex constructions of unique_ptr and shared_ptr might not be because C++ allows arbitrary order of evaluation of subexpressions (until C++17).
recommendation: Review and fix according to the rule guidelines.

checks.message: Use make_unique/make_shared instead of direct construction with new for better exception safety.
